memberdef.definition: using faiss::simd_result_handlers::RangeHandler< C, with_id_map, SL >::T = typename C::T
memberdef.name: T

memberdef.definition: using faiss::simd_result_handlers::RangeHandler< C, with_id_map, SL >::TI = typename C::TI
memberdef.name: TI

memberdef.definition: using faiss::simd_result_handlers::RangeHandler< C, with_id_map, SL >::RHC = ResultHandlerCompare<C, with_id_map, SL>
memberdef.name: RHC

briefdescription.para: these fields are used mainly for the IVF variants (with_id_map=true)

memberdef.argsstring: (RangeSearchResult &rres, float radius, size_t ntotal, const IDSelector *sel_in)
memberdef.name: RangeHandler

memberdef.argsstring: (size_t q, size_t b, simd16uint16 d0, simd16uint16 d1) final
memberdef.name: handle

briefdescription.para: set the sub-matrix that is being computed

memberdef.argsstring: (uint16_t thr, size_t b, simd16uint16 d0, simd16uint16 d1)
memberdef.name: get_lt_mask

detaileddescription.para: return binary mask of elements below thr in (d0, d1) inverse_test returns elements above
Set context information for handlers that need additional data
This method can be overridden by handlers that need list numbers and probe mappings (e.g., RaBitQ handlers). Base implementation does nothing since most handlers don't need this context.

parameterdescription.para: current inverted list number being processed
mapping from local query index to probe index

memberdef.argsstring: (size_t q, size_t b, simd16uint16 d0, simd16uint16 d1)=0
memberdef.name: handle

detaileddescription.para: called when 32 distances are computed and provided in two simd16uint16. (q, b) indicate which entry it is in the block.
Result handler for range search. The difficulty is that the range distances have to be scaled using the scaler.
